trigger_conditions: contradiction engine reports gap between world model and observed filesystem state
ContradictionEngine.scan() returns severity=medium or severity=high for a file entity
world model declares a file entity that does not match filesystem observation

reusable_steps.description: Run contradiction engine scan to confirm gap
reusable_steps.verification: contradiction_engine.scan() returns the specific entity with gap confirmed

reusable_steps.description: Correct the divergent state — update file or update world model declaration
reusable_steps.verification: file or declaration matches the expected state

reusable_steps.description: Re-scan with contradiction engine to confirm gap is cleared
reusable_steps.verification: contradiction_engine.scan() returns severity=none for the entity

validation.description: Re-run contradiction engine scan — entity must return severity=none. Verify world model observation matches filesystem state.

rollback.description: Revert the file edit using git checkout -- <path>. If world model declaration was changed, restore prior declaration value.

known_failure_modes: entity has multiple contradictions — resolving one reveals another
world model re-scan not triggered after edit
expected_outcome: Contradiction engine reports severity=none for the target entity

policy_checks: risk_is_low=true, template_exists=true, validation_exists=true, rollback_or_non_mutating=true, has_evidence=true — all passed

trigger_conditions: readiness model reports a dimension score below 0.70 threshold
dimension score has been below threshold for 2+ consecutive cadence cycles
improvement action for the dimension is classified as low risk

reusable_steps.description: Query readiness model for current dimension scores
reusable_steps.verification: readiness dimension score retrieved and below 0.70

reusable_steps.description: Execute the targeted improvement for the weakest dimension
reusable_steps.verification: improvement action completed without error

reusable_steps.description: Re-query readiness model to confirm score improved
reusable_steps.verification: dimension score >= 0.70 or shows measurable improvement toward threshold

validation.description: Re-query readiness model — target dimension score must show measurable improvement. If Reliability dimension: re-run module imports and verify all pass.

rollback.description: If file was modified: git checkout -- <path>. Readiness model re-scans automatically on next cadence cycle.

known_failure_modes: improvement raises one dimension but lowers another
dimension score requires multiple steps to reach threshold
expected_outcome: Target readiness dimension score >= 0.70 or measurable improvement toward threshold

evidence.detail: agent_reliability_before=0.889, template_confidence=1.0, execution_status=partial, validation_result=partial — readiness scoring live during execution

trigger_conditions: world model observation declares a file path that does not exist on filesystem
observation accuracy check returns path_exists=false for a declared entity
declared path is a configuration path or data store path, not generated output

reusable_steps.description: Confirm declared path does not exist on filesystem
reusable_steps.verification: os.path.exists(declared_path) returns False

reusable_steps.description: Create the missing path or correct the declaration to match reality
reusable_steps.verification: path exists on filesystem OR declaration updated to correct path

reusable_steps.description: Trigger world model re-observation for the entity
reusable_steps.verification: world model entity observation shows path_exists=true

validation.description: Verify os.path.exists(declared_path) returns True. Re-run world model observation for the entity — observation must show path_exists=true.

rollback.description: If file was created: os.remove(path). If declaration was changed: git checkout -- <config_file>.

known_failure_modes: path correction reveals a missing upstream file that must also be created
declaration is in a non-editable generated config
expected_outcome: World model entity observation shows path_exists=true for the declared path

trigger_conditions: world model observation for an entity is stale — last_observed > 24h ago
filesystem state has changed but world model has not been re-scanned
readiness model signals a freshness gap for a specific entity type

reusable_steps.description: Identify stale entity and verify current filesystem state
reusable_steps.verification: entity identified, current filesystem state confirmed

reusable_steps.description: Trigger world model re-observation for the stale entity
reusable_steps.verification: world model entity last_observed timestamp updated to current time

reusable_steps.description: Verify coherence propagation fired for dependent entities
reusable_steps.verification: propagation events fired for downstream entities

validation.description: World model entity.last_observed is within last 2 hours. Coherence propagation log shows propagation events for the entity.

rollback.description: World model observations are non-destructive reads. No rollback required — re-observation cannot harm system state.

known_failure_modes: entity scan raises import error on first use
propagation loop re-queues stale entities faster than scan resolves them
expected_outcome: World model entity is fresh — last_observed within 2 hours, all dependent entities propagated

trigger_conditions: api response shape does not match the declared contract for an endpoint
cockpit truth matrix reports a field present in contract but missing from actual response
production truth verification shows file_divergence=true for an api module

reusable_steps.description: Retrieve declared contract and actual response for the endpoint
reusable_steps.verification: contract fields enumerated, actual response sample captured

reusable_steps.description: Edit api module to align response shape with contract
reusable_steps.verification: all contract fields present in modified response handler

reusable_steps.description: Verify endpoint response matches contract via live probe
reusable_steps.verification: curl or requests call returns all declared contract fields

validation.description: Live endpoint probe returns all fields declared in contract. python3 -m py_compile on modified api module passes. Production truth verification shows file_divergence=false.

rollback.description: git checkout -- <api_module_path>. docker restart <service_name> to restore prior behavior.

known_failure_modes: response shape change breaks a frontend component
rollback requires service restart
expected_outcome: Endpoint response matches declared contract — all required fields present, no undeclared fields

evidence.detail: affected_subsystems: [production_merge_verifier, cockpit_routes, security_hardening] — all verified post-merge

trigger_conditions: pytest reports a failing test assertion for a feature that is otherwise functional
test failure is an assertion mismatch — expected vs actual value divergence
test file imports compile cleanly but the assertion fails at runtime

reusable_steps.description: Run failing test in isolation to confirm failure and capture exact error
reusable_steps.verification: pytest -xvs <test_file>::<test_fn> shows AssertionError with expected vs actual values

reusable_steps.description: Read test file and production module to understand the assertion drift
reusable_steps.verification: root cause identified: assertion expects old behavior, production changed

reusable_steps.description: Update test assertion to match current correct behavior — do NOT change production code
reusable_steps.verification: test file edited, assertion updated

reusable_steps.description: Re-run test suite to confirm pass
reusable_steps.verification: pytest -xvs <test_file>::<test_fn> passes with exit code 0

validation.description: pytest <test_file>::<test_fn> exits with code 0. Prior passing tests in same file still pass. Production module py_compile unchanged.

rollback.description: git checkout -- <test_file_path>. Run pytest to confirm prior (failing) state restored.

evidence_requirements: failing pytest output with assertion error
test file path and function name
known_failure_modes: test repair reveals a real production bug (assertion was correct)
fixing one test breaks another in same file
expected_outcome: Failing test passes. All other tests in file continue to pass. No production code modified.

evidence.detail: validation_summary.new_tests=79, prior_tests_passing=1159 — test suite maintained after new module additions
manifest.validation_results: pytest test_phase9_7_pr_factory.py passed=true, exit_code=0, output_summary='79 passed'

trigger_conditions: cockpit API endpoint for a substrate feature returns 404 or is missing entirely
substrate module is production_ready but has no cockpit surface (no /api/umh/ route)
cockpit truth matrix reports missing_endpoint=true for a feature

reusable_steps.description: Confirm endpoint is missing and identify target cockpit route file
reusable_steps.verification: curl /api/umh/<endpoint> returns 404, target route file identified

reusable_steps.description: Add GET route handler to appropriate cockpit route module
reusable_steps.verification: route handler added, py_compile passes, router mounts new route

reusable_steps.description: Probe new endpoint to verify response contract
reusable_steps.verification: curl /api/umh/<endpoint> returns 200 with expected response fields

validation.description: curl /api/umh/<endpoint> returns HTTP 200 with all expected fields. py_compile on modified cockpit route file passes. No existing routes return 404 after change.

rollback.description: git checkout -- <cockpit_route_file>. docker restart os-operator to restore prior routing.

evidence_requirements: cockpit truth matrix showing missing endpoint
known_failure_modes: new route conflicts with existing route pattern
service restart required to pick up route change
expected_outcome: Cockpit surface exposes substrate feature — endpoint returns valid response, truth matrix no longer shows missing_endpoint

evidence.detail: mode_after=dry_run_only, set_mode_response={ok: true, mode: dry_run_only} — cockpit endpoint successfully activated cadence mode
safety_verification.cockpit_shows_last_dry_run=true — cockpit panel correctly surfaced dry-run result

trigger_conditions: python module exceeds 3000 lines — pre-commit line count gate would block commit
module contains multiple route groups that can be split into dedicated router modules
each route group has a common URL prefix or concern that groups naturally

reusable_steps.description: Count lines and identify route group boundaries in the over-limit file
reusable_steps.verification: wc -l <file> confirms line count, route groups identified by URL prefix

reusable_steps.description: Extract one route group to a new dedicated router module
reusable_steps.verification: new router file created, routes imported and mounted in original file, py_compile passes

reusable_steps.description: Verify all extracted routes still respond identically
reusable_steps.verification: all previously-accessible endpoints return same status codes and response shapes

validation.description: wc -l <original_file> shows fewer lines after extraction. py_compile on both original and new router module passes. All routes previously accessible remain accessible at same paths.

rollback.description: git checkout -- <original_file> && git rm <new_router_file>. docker restart service to restore prior routing.

evidence_requirements: list of routes being extracted with their URL prefixes
known_failure_modes: extracted routes import symbols from original file that create circular imports
router mounting order matters and extraction changes evaluation order
expected_outcome: Original file is below 3000 lines. All routes work identically. New router module is importable.

evidence.detail: manifest.changed_files includes transports/api/cockpit.py (modified, +130/-0) and 7 other files — route extraction pattern with minimal removal
manifest.risk_proof: no_auth_changes=true, no_destructive_ops=true, file_count_within_limit=true — extraction completed safely

trigger_conditions: dependency graph is older than 24 hours — session_bootstrap.py reports stale
query_graph.py search returns missing results for a recently-added module
graph node summary does not include a file added in the last commit
required_context: list of files added or modified since last graph build

reusable_steps.description: Confirm graph staleness and identify new/modified files
reusable_steps.verification: python3 scripts/session_bootstrap.py --compact exits non-zero, new files not in graph

reusable_steps.description: Rebuild dependency graph
reusable_steps.verification: scripts/update-graph exits 0, no errors

reusable_steps.description: Verify graph is fresh and new files are indexed
reusable_steps.verification: python3 scripts/session_bootstrap.py --compact exits 0, query_graph.py search finds newly-added files

validation.description: python3 scripts/session_bootstrap.py --compact exits 0. python3 scripts/query_graph.py search <new_module_name> returns the file.

rollback.description: Graph rebuild is non-destructive — it reads source files and writes data/. No rollback required. Previous graph files are overwritten, not source code.

known_failure_modes: graph rebuild takes > 2 minutes on large codebases
new file has syntax error that blocks graph indexing
expected_outcome: Dependency graph is fresh — session_bootstrap reports no staleness, all recent files are queryable

evidence.detail: propagation.wave_2_targets includes dependency_graph_recompute — graph rebuild triggered as wave-2 propagation after production truth promotion

trigger_conditions: organism health probe detects a degraded component that requires a non-file-edit maintenance action
docker container for a service shows unhealthy status or non-zero restarts
log file for a service shows repeated recoverable errors over last hour

reusable_steps.description: Run health probes to identify degraded component
reusable_steps.verification: docker ps shows unhealthy or non-zero restarts, log error pattern confirmed

reusable_steps.description: Execute targeted maintenance action for the degraded component
reusable_steps.verification: maintenance action completed without error

reusable_steps.description: Re-run health probes to confirm component is healthy
reusable_steps.verification: docker ps shows healthy status, log errors no longer appearing

validation.description: docker ps shows healthy status for affected service after maintenance. Log file shows no new error occurrences for the pattern that triggered the action.

rollback.description: Maintenance actions that modify config files: git checkout -- <config>. Service restarts: docker restart <service_name> to return to prior state.

evidence_requirements: health probe output showing degraded state before action
known_failure_modes: maintenance action resolves symptom but not root cause — issue recurs
service restart causes brief downtime for dependent components
expected_outcome: Organism health probe shows all components healthy after maintenance action

evidence.detail: lane_status: total_runs=2, completed_runs=1, failed_runs=0 — maintenance runs completed without failure
policy: require_rollback_or_non_mutating=true, require_validation=true — all maintenance candidates gated by rollback and validation requirements

trigger_conditions: module docstring references a stale or renamed project name
code comment contains outdated terminology that no longer matches the codebase
documentation file has factual inaccuracies relative to current code state

reusable_steps.description: Identify all instances of the stale reference in the target file
reusable_steps.verification: grep confirms all stale reference locations identified

reusable_steps.description: Replace stale references with current correct terminology in docstrings only
reusable_steps.verification: diff shows only docstring or comment changes, no logic changes

reusable_steps.description: Verify file still compiles after documentation change
reusable_steps.verification: py_compile succeeds on modified file

validation.description: py_compile on modified file succeeds. grep for stale reference returns zero matches in modified file. No logic or import changes — only docstring or comment text.

rollback.description: git checkout -- <file_path>. Non-destructive: only docstring text was changed.

known_failure_modes: stale name appears in a string literal that is used programmatically, not just documentation
expected_outcome: Stale project names replaced with current terminology. No logic changes. File compiles.

evidence.detail: Projection boundary cleanup (phase 2026-05-28) established naming rules; stale references remain in scripts/
